description: Watch cache sends DELETE events with PrevObject's stale resourceVersion instead of the event's current resourceVersion, causing informers to track wrong version

root_cause.summary: In the switch statement that converts watch events based on filter matching, the case for DELETE events (!curObjPasses && oldObjPasses) uses event.PrevObject.DeepCopyObject() which retains the previous resourceVersion. The event's actual resourceVersion should be set on the returned object. The etcd3/watcher.go and etcd/etcd_watcher.go have the correct logic.
root_cause.mechanism: The watch cache filtering logic: case !curObjPasses && oldObjPasses: watchEvent = watch.Event{Type: watch.Deleted, Object: event.PrevObject.DeepCopyObject()} -- this sends the old object's content with the old resourceVersion. It should set the event's resourceVersion on the copy.

error_chain.action: Establishes namespace-scoped watch, tracks resourceVersion from events
BUG: Converts Modified->Deleted event using PrevObject with stale resourceVersion
Correctly handles resourceVersion on delete events (reference implementation)

remediation: After constructing the DELETE watch event from PrevObject.DeepCopyObject(), set the event's resourceVersion on the copied object using the versioner. This matches the existing logic in etcd3/watcher.go (lines 373-378) and etcd/etcd_watcher.go (lines 401-403).
